text:
HEMATOLOGY / BONE MARROW TRANSPLANT PROGRAM
CONSULTATION NOTE -- ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT EVALUATION

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Attending BMT Physician]
FELLOW: [BMT Fellow]
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [Leukemia Attending]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR CONSULTATION:
Ms. Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed ELN Adverse-risk acute myeloid leukemia (AML) -- FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, complex co-mutation profile -- who achieved complete remission (CR) with MRD negativity after one cycle of 7+3 plus midostaurin induction chemotherapy. She is referred for evaluation and transplant planning in the setting of CR1, with the goal of proceeding to allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT).

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female who presented to her primary care physician approximately 10 weeks ago with a 5-week history of progressive fatigue, exertional dyspnea, easy bruising, spontaneous gingival bleeding, night sweats, and approximately 7 pounds of unintentional weight loss. On initial evaluation, she was found to have severe leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/uL) with 42% circulating blasts on peripheral smear, profound anemia (hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (platelets 28,000/uL). She was emergently admitted to the hematology-oncology service.

Bone marrow biopsy at diagnosis showed a markedly hypercellular marrow (estimated 95% cellularity) replaced by 74% myeloblasts with Auer rods. Flow cytometry was consistent with AML (CD34+, CD117+, CD13+, CD33+, MPO+, HLA-DR+). Conventional cytogenetics revealed trisomy 8 as the sole clonal cytogenetic abnormality (46,XX,+8[12]/46,XX[8]). FISH was negative for core-binding factor rearrangements and PML-RARA. Comprehensive NGS panel (468-gene hematologic malignancy panel) confirmed a high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD (VAF 48.2%, allelic ratio 0.91 -- adverse prognosis), along with the following co-mutations: DNMT3A R882H (VAF 49.8%, likely CHIP-origin founder clone), TET2 Q1299* (VAF 52.1%), WT1 R462W (VAF 31.4%, potential MRD marker), NRAS Q61H (VAF 18.7%, subclonal), KDM6A S1025fs (VAF 44.3%), STAG2 E253* (VAF 21.6%), and BCOR G1522fs (VAF 14.2%). NPM1, IDH1/IDH2, TP53, CEBPA, and KRAS were all negative. ELN 2022 risk classification: Adverse (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio with NPM1 wild-type, further compounded by DNMT3A R882H and the complex multi-hit co-mutation landscape).

She was treated with standard 7+3 induction: cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day CIV days 1-7 plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV push days 1-3, with midostaurin 50 mg PO BID added on days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol. Her induction course was complicated by Grade 3 febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia (treated successfully with cefepime), Grade 2 mucositis requiring opioid analgesia, and expected transfusion dependence. She did not develop clinical tumor lysis syndrome. Cardiac monitoring showed no significant QTc prolongation with midostaurin (max QTc 438 ms).

Day +35 bone marrow biopsy showed a hypocellular marrow (10-15% cellularity) with trilineage hematopoiesis and no morphologic residual AML (blasts <3%). Multi-parameter flow cytometry MRD assay (10-color, sensitivity 0.01%) was negative. FLT3-ITD PCR was below the limit of detection (sensitivity 10^-4). WT1 PCR: 26 copies/10^4 ABL1 copies (normal range <50). CBC at day 35 showed WBC 3,200/uL, ANC 1,850/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, platelets 112,000/uL. She was seen in outpatient leukemia clinic at day 35 in good condition; ECOG PS 1. She was counseled regarding the strong recommendation for allo-HSCT consolidation given her adverse-risk disease profile, and she agreed to proceed with transplant evaluation. HLA typing was sent at the time of diagnosis.

Pre-transplant workup CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (performed approximately 6 weeks post-induction) showed interval improvement in splenomegaly (now 10.8 cm, previously 16.4 cm at diagnosis), with no lymphadenopathy, no hepatic lesions, and no evidence of myeloid sarcoma or extramedullary disease. Echocardiogram showed LVEF 62% with no significant valvular disease and no wall motion abnormalities. Pulmonary function tests: FEV1 88% predicted, DLCO 84% predicted -- both within acceptable range for myeloablative conditioning.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Hypothyroidism -- managed with levothyroxine; TSH and free T4 within normal limits on stable dose at last check.
2. Mild hypertension -- well-controlled; on low-dose antihypertensive therapy.
3. AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN Adverse risk) -- as above; in CR1 with MRD negativity.
4. No prior hematologic malignancy or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
5. No prior solid organ malignancy.
6. No prior significant cardiac or pulmonary disease.
7. No prior hepatic or renal disease.
8. No prior autoimmune conditions.
9. No prior serious infections beyond induction-related bacteremia (see above).

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior major surgical procedures reported.
- No prior chemotherapy or radiation therapy prior to this AML diagnosis.

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother: hypertension, type 2 diabetes. Deceased (natural causes).
- Father: no known malignancy. Hypertension.
- Maternal grandmother: breast cancer (postmenopausal, treated in her 70s).
- No known first-degree relatives with AML, MDS, or other hematologic malignancies.
- No known familial cancer syndrome.
- Siblings: one sibling (brother); reportedly healthy; no HLA typing performed (patient does not wish to burden family member with directed donation evaluation at this time; unrelated donor available).

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Occupation: High school biology teacher, currently on medical leave of absence since diagnosis.
- Tobacco: Never smoker.
- Alcohol: Occasional social use, less than 1 drink per week.
- Illicit substances: Denied.
- Marital status: Married; supportive husband at home. Two adult children, both healthy.
- Living situation: Lives in a house with her husband; adequate support system for the post-transplant outpatient recovery period.
- Insurance: Verified; transplant authorization in process.
- Infectious disease exposure: No recent travel to endemic fungal areas. No animal exposure or construction/renovation activity. No known TB exposure.
- CMV serostatus: Positive (CMV IgG positive).
- EBV serostatus: Positive.

ALLERGIES:
- No known drug allergies. No known food or environmental allergies reported.

MEDICATIONS (current):
1. Levothyroxine 88 mcg orally once daily (hypothyroidism).
2. Amlodipine 5 mg orally once daily (hypertension).
3. Acyclovir 400 mg orally twice daily (viral prophylaxis, post-induction).
4. Fluconazole 200 mg orally once daily (antifungal prophylaxis, post-induction; to be reassessed pre-conditioning).
5. Ciprofloxacin prophylaxis -- recently completed per protocol (induction-related antimicrobial prophylaxis course).
6. Ondansetron 8 mg orally as needed (nausea; being used infrequently at this point).
7. Iron supplementation / folate (nutritional support during recovery).
8. Calcium carbonate 1000 mg daily and vitamin D 1000 IU daily (initiated per PCP recommendation).

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Reports mild fatigue that is improving week over week. No current fevers, chills, or night sweats. Appetite is recovering; back to approximately 80% of normal intake. Has regained approximately 3 of the 7 pounds lost prior to diagnosis.
- HEENT: No oral sores or mucositis at this visit. No visual changes. No epistaxis.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
- Pulmonary: Mild exertional dyspnea with sustained activity; significantly improved compared to initial presentation. No cough, hemoptysis.
- Gastrointestinal: Appetite improving. Bowel movements regular. No nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea at present.
- Genitourinary: No dysuria, hematuria.
- Musculoskeletal: Generalized deconditioning and mild fatigue-related weakness; no focal joint pain, bone pain, or myalgias.
- Neurologic: No headache, focal weakness, or sensory changes. No cognitive changes noted.
- Skin: No new rash, bruising, or petechiae noted. Residual mild skin dryness in areas of prior petechiae.
- Endocrine: Thyroid symptoms well-controlled.
- Psychiatric: Acknowledges anxiety about the transplant process and long-term prognosis. Interested in speaking with a social worker and/or transplant psychologist. Overall mood is appropriate; no signs of clinical depression or anxiety disorder requiring formal intervention at this time.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
- General: Well-appearing female in no acute distress. Appears well-nourished but mildly fatigued. Alert and fully oriented.
- Vital signs: Blood pressure 122/76 mmHg, heart rate 74 bpm, respiratory rate 16/min, temperature 36.7 degrees Celsius, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight: 68 kg. Height: 165 cm. BSA (Mosteller): approximately 1.77 m2.
- HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Anicteric sclerae. Conjunctivae pink and moist. Oropharynx: oral mucosa intact, moist, no ulcerations, no thrush. No active gingival bleeding. Dentition adequate.
- Lymph nodes: No palpable cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy.
- Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No lower extremity edema.
- Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
- Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Liver palpable approximately 1 cm below the right costal margin (compared to 17.1 cm hepatomegaly at diagnosis). Spleen: not palpably enlarged on this examination (previously 3 cm below left costal margin; resolved consistent with imaging). No ascites.
- Skin: No petechiae, no ecchymoses, no rash. Mild residual hyperpigmented patches on bilateral lower extremities consistent with prior petechiae.
- Neurologic: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Gait normal.
- Performance status: ECOG 1. Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS): 90%.

---

LAB RESULTS (most recent, pre-transplant evaluation):
- WBC: 4,800/uL | ANC: 2,900/uL | Hemoglobin: 10.6 g/dL | Platelets: 168,000/uL
- Comprehensive metabolic panel: Creatinine 0.82 mg/dL (eGFR >60). BUN 14 mg/dL. Sodium 138 mEq/L. Potassium 4.1 mEq/L. Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L. Glucose 96 mg/dL. Total bilirubin 0.5 mg/dL. AST 28 U/L. ALT 32 U/L. Alkaline phosphatase 68 U/L. Albumin 3.8 g/dL.
- Coagulation: PT 12.4 sec, INR 1.0, aPTT 28 sec. Fibrinogen 318 mg/dL.
- LDH: 178 U/L (normal).
- Ferritin: 1,840 ng/mL (elevated, likely related to transfusion support and disease-related hyperferritinemia; being monitored).
- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal range on levothyroxine).
- Hepatitis B surface antigen: Negative. Hepatitis B core antibody: Negative. Hepatitis B surface antibody: Positive (immune, prior vaccination). Hepatitis C antibody: Negative. HIV Ag/Ab: Negative.
- CMV IgG: Positive (patient seropositive). CMV PCR: Not detected.
- EBV IgG: Positive. EBV PCR: Not detected.
- Toxoplasma IgG: Negative.
- Urine analysis: Within normal limits, no proteinuria.
- FLT3-ITD PCR (peripheral blood, most recent): Not detected.
- Peripheral blood chimerism (if applicable): Not yet assessed (pre-transplant baseline; patient's own cells).

HLA TYPING (patient):
- HLA-A*02:01, A*24:02
- HLA-B*07:02, B*44:03
- HLA-C*07:02, C*16:01
- HLA-DRB1*01:01, DRB1*15:01
- HLA-DQB1*05:01, DQB1*06:02
- (10-locus allele-level typing complete)

DONOR SEARCH:
- Bone marrow registry search identified a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD): 34-year-old male. CMV serostatus: positive (CMV D+/R+ -- highest risk, letermovir prophylaxis planned). Blood group compatibility: to be confirmed with donor typing.
- Haploidentical donor option not actively pursued given availability of well-matched MUD.
- No HLA-matched sibling identified (sibling not typed; patient preference).
- Cord blood options reviewed but deemed suboptimal given adult weight and available MUD quality.

CARDIOPULMONARY ASSESSMENT:
- Echocardiogram: LVEF 62%, no significant valvular disease, no wall motion abnormalities, no pericardial effusion.
- Pulmonary function tests: FEV1 88% predicted, DLCO 84% predicted. No significant restriction or obstruction. Adequate for myeloablative conditioning.

---

IMAGING:
- Pre-transplant CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (performed approximately 6 weeks post-induction): Interval improvement in splenomegaly (10.8 cm vs. 16.4 cm at diagnosis). No lymphadenopathy. No hepatic lesions. No evidence of myeloid sarcoma or extramedullary disease. No pleural or pericardial effusion.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with ELN 2022 Adverse-risk AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio 0.91, NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, DNMT3A R882H, TET2, WT1, and multiple additional co-mutations) who achieved MRD-negative CR1 after a single cycle of 7+3 plus midostaurin. She has recovered well from induction, with near-normalization of counts, resolution of splenomegaly, and ECOG PS 1 with KPS 90%.

She presents for transplant evaluation and is deemed a candidate for allo-HSCT in CR1.

1. TRANSPLANT INDICATION AND RATIONALE:
   The indication for allo-HSCT in CR1 is well-supported. Her adverse-risk disease features -- high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD in an NPM1 wild-type context, trisomy 8, and a complex co-mutation landscape including DNMT3A R882H and TET2 (often referred to as a "CHIP-origin" AML, known to carry inferior outcomes) -- place her at very high risk of relapse with chemotherapy alone. Multiple retrospective and prospective datasets, as well as NCCN and ELN guidelines, support allo-HSCT consolidation in CR1 for this molecular profile. Her MRD-negative status going into transplant is the most favorable prognostic factor we can bring to the procedure.

2. HCT COMORBIDITY INDEX AND FITNESS:
   HCT-CI: 1 (hypothyroidism, managed, no organ dysfunction). Cardiopulmonary function: acceptable for myeloablative conditioning. Renal and hepatic function: within normal limits. KPS 90%, ECOG PS 1. She is fit for myeloablative conditioning (MAC).

3. CONDITIONING REGIMEN:
   Given her age (48, within typical MAC range at most institutions), absence of major comorbidities, and the high-risk disease biology (where a stronger graft-versus-leukemia effect and deeper marrow ablation are desirable), we recommend MYELOABLATIVE CONDITIONING (MAC) with fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4):
   - Fludarabine 40 mg/m2/day IV x 4 days (days -6 through -3)
   - Busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day IV x 4 days (days -6 through -3) with pharmacokinetic monitoring and dose adjustment to target AUC 4800-6000 micromol*min/L
   This regimen provides robust myeloablation while the pharmacokinetic monitoring of busulfan minimizes the risk of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS/VOD) and excessive extramedullary toxicity.

4. DONOR:
   Proceed with 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (34-year-old male, CMV seropositive). This is an excellent match; single-antigen mismatch or less is the standard threshold. Peripheral blood stem cells (PBSC) will be requested as the graft source to optimize engraftment kinetics.

5. GRAFT-VERSUS-HOST DISEASE PROPHYLAXIS:
   Tacrolimus (target trough 10-15 ng/mL starting day -3) plus short-course methotrexate (MTX 15 mg/m2 IV day +1; 10 mg/m2 IV days +3 and +6) per our institutional standard for MUD transplant. Post-transplant cyclophosphamide was discussed but tacrolimus/MTX remains our standard for 10/10 MUD in the MAC setting at this center.

6. INFECTION PROPHYLAXIS:
   - Acyclovir: Continue through at least day +365 (standard herpes/VZV prophylaxis).
   - Letermovir 480 mg PO daily starting day +1 through day +100 (CMV prophylaxis, given D+/R+ status).
   - Fluconazole: Continue through engraftment; reassess based on clinical status.
   - Levofloxacin: Antibacterial prophylaxis during neutropenic period.
   - TMP-SMX: PCP prophylaxis to begin after engraftment.

7. SUPPORTIVE CARE:
   Transfusion support as per institutional protocol. G-CSF post-transplant not routinely used with PBSC graft; will reassess based on engraftment kinetics. Nutritional consultation pre-admission. Social work referral completed (patient seen today; support resources provided). Psychology consultation will be offered.

8. ADDITIONAL CONSOLIDATION PRIOR TO TRANSPLANT:
   NOT recommended. Given adequate marrow recovery, donor availability, MRD-negative CR1 status, and no evidence of extramedullary disease, proceeding directly to transplant without additional consolidation is appropriate and avoids unnecessary toxicity and the risk of disease recurrence during inter-treatment interval.

9. REPRODUCTIVE COUNSELING:
   The patient is 48 years old. She has been counseled that allo-HSCT with myeloablative conditioning will result in permanent gonadal failure and infertility. She and her husband were seen together and she reports she has completed childbearing. She was offered formal reproductive endocrinology consultation and declined, stating she understood the permanent nature of treatment-related menopause. Hormone replacement therapy options were briefly reviewed.

10. TIMELINE:
    Target transplant admission in approximately 2-3 weeks pending donor mobilization scheduling and completion of remaining logistics. Dental clearance requested. Central venous access (tunneled catheter or PICC) to be placed prior to conditioning initiation.

The patient and her husband were present during this consultation. All aspects of the transplant procedure, including risks (engraftment failure, infections, acute and chronic GVHD, organ toxicity, secondary malignancy, relapse), benefits (graft-versus-leukemia effect, potential for cure), and alternatives (additional chemotherapy cycles without transplant, enrollment on clinical trial if available) were discussed in detail. The patient expressed understanding of the rationale for proceeding and wishes to go forward with allo-HSCT. She has an excellent support system and appropriate understanding of the extended recovery timeline.

Signed: [BMT Attending Physician]
Co-signed: [BMT Fellow]
